title: Kashmiri Pulao
description: A fragrant Kashmiri rice dish prepared with premium basmati rice, aromatic whole spices, saffron, and a royal garnish of fruits and nuts. Perfect for festive meals and special occasions.

ingredients.title: For the Rice
items: Basmati rice – 1½ cups (aged, long-grain, preferably premium reserve)

ingredients.title: For the Flavored Base
items: Saffron strands – 12–15, soaked in 2 tbsp warm milk
Kashmiri dry ginger powder – ¼ tsp (optional but authentic)
Fennel powder – ½ tsp (optional but recommended)

ingredients.title: Fruits & Nuts (Traditional Garnish)
items: Pineapple cubes – ½ cup (fresh or lightly stewed)
Apple – ½, diced (optional but gives royal touch)

ingredients.title: Sweetness & Balance
items: White pepper powder – ¼ tsp (optional for mild heat)

instruction.title: Step 1: Prepare the Rice
steps: Wash basmati gently 3–4 times until water runs clear.
Boil water with salt and 2 tbsp ghee. Add rice, cook until 90% done (grain still has a bite).
Drain and spread rice gently to avoid sticking.

instruction.title: Step 2: Prepare Garnish
steps: Heat 1 tbsp ghee in a small pan.
Lightly fry cashews, almonds, then raisins until plump.
Remove and set aside on a paper towel.

instruction.title: Step 3: Build the Flavored Base
steps: In a heavy-bottom pan, heat 2 tbsp ghee.
Add cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf — sauté 30 seconds until aromatic.
Add onions, sauté until translucent and lightly golden (not deeply browned).
Add ginger juliennes, fennel powder, and dry ginger powder — sauté briefly.
Stir in saffron milk and sugar; mix well.

instruction.title: Step 4: Assemble the Pulao
steps: Gently fold the cooked rice into the spiced base.
Add pineapple cubes and a pinch more salt if needed.
Cover and cook on dum (very low heat) for 5 minutes so flavors infuse without breaking grains.

instruction.title: Step 5: Final Touch & Plating
steps: Fluff the rice gently with a fork.
Transfer to a shallow serving dish.
Scatter fried nuts, raisins, pomegranate arils, and apple cubes on top.
Serve warm — pairs beautifully with mild gravies like Shahi Paneer, Korma, or Nadur Yakhni.

servingSuggestions.title: Serving Suggestions
items: Pair with Korma or Nadur Yakhni.
Garnish with fried nuts, raisins, pomegranate arils, and fresh apple cubes before serving.

chefTips: Rice Texture: Use aged basmati and avoid overcooking; every grain should remain separate.
Fruit Freshness: For restaurant plating, keep pomegranate and apple raw for a burst of freshness, but use lightly stewed pineapple for sweetness without tartness.
Color Balance: Saffron should tint the rice pale gold — avoid excessive turmeric, which is not traditional here.
Luxury Factor: For high-end presentation, you can serve in a copper handi with the lid sealed by dough for tableside reveal.
